reviews.review_msg: He is knowledgeble but not a good peron, he made everything so complicated and difficult. Not recommend!
He was one of the bad profs during my 4 years. A lot of the things he taught was outdated, and he forced us to participate in class discussions.
He has been giving lectures on environmental protection topics, making already dull knowledge even more complicated. Highly not recommend.
After taking his 2 sessions, I chose to drop it. His class structure is so confused and repeats so many common sense... Not recommend!
He made everything so complicated! Not actual teach anything and just spend your money to draw trees with him, have fun everyone...
His lecture slides are images with little text. He has a 30% midterm with in-class discussions and other random stuff. Don't take BUS 275 with him.

I dont know if theres another option for a prof, if there is take the other option. Might be the most boring/ scattered lecture I have ever attended. Thanks SFU for another mandatory bs class to give someone a charity job. Who actually cares about sustainability
Dr. Kent Williams is very knowledgeable, caring and supportive professor! I had one  of my best experience during my MBA journey at SFU.

It seems the undergrads don't appreciate Kent. But if you're taking the MBA or GDBA, make sure to take his course!!! It is truly transformative. I absolutely loved it. For the more mature students who care deeply about this world and earth, you will enjoy learning from Kent. A pity for those who don't and who only care about their letter grades.

He is passionate and knowledgeble about sustainability, but he did not follow the textbook to teach, which was free teaching with no order at all. 200 Level should not be like this; he needs to change the way he teaches (especially on the exam).
DO NOT TAKE HIS BUS275!!!

His 275 is not a good choice to take with you; the contents students reviewed were not on the exam. Even little in-class discussion points are all elaborated on in the exam, which means EXTREMELY DIFFICULT!
I loved his class he is my favorite professor of all time
Friendly professor, however the weekly tasks given I felt were pointless. We had to write long reflections on how much we love Earth. I don't see how this relates to business.
Challenging exam; people who complain are not kidding! All the exam contents are the class discussions. DO NOT TAKE BUS275 WITH HIM!!!!!!!!!! (HONEST ADVICE)
RUN RUN RUN!!! He is not good to teach this course, really hard!!!
HARD MT EXAM!! HARD MT EXAM!! HARD MT EXAM!! HARD MT EXAM!!

He had a VERY HARD midterm exam; it was really not a good choice to take this course with him! It is understandable to recognize the course is not easy; he made the test really really really hard to prepare for. Choose others instead!
He is okay! But his exam was really challenging and not acceptable; it is not fine to take it with the first-year freshman.

He's nice, but his Canvas and criteria are unorganized, and content mostly repeats itself the whole semester. It can be really boring by week 4. He does weekly in-person open-book quizzes based on readings, and the only exam is focused on specific things in his slides + readings. Relate course material to all writing assignments and you'll do fine.

I honestly really liked this class. The final assignment was kind of confusing, and we had to clarify with our TA a lot. His lecture style is definitely unique; he makes his lectures speeches but I liked it. All content talked about in the lecture is straight from the readings so his lectures just help you think more critically about the content.

I thought Dr. Williams was a kind and fair prof! If you do the required material—readings and short tedtalks—you will do well on the midterm and overall in the class (even if u just glance over the long ones). The content is not difficult and I thought the modules were well organized with great real-world examples to enhance the business aspect.

This course was on the more challenging side, but overall found it's helped me reshape how I study and take notes. Kent is kind, and truly cares about the topics he brings up in lectures. Please pay attention to what he speaks about in class though- its often brought up again on tests!!!

Dr. Williams is a friendly guy, and you can tell he is passionate about his topic of teaching. Although people on here speak of how this is a difficult course, I think it is worth it to take this with Kent as he will expand your learning and critical thinking skills.
You could imagine how his rate for teaching courses was his first time at SFU. Just run and choose another prof...
If you want to get a super challenging exam, choose him without any doubt, and I wish you can successfully pass this course. Good luck to his future students...

He is not clear with what he wants, and your grade will reflect that. All he talks about in lectures are things that won't even be applied later on in the course. It is so useless to study this course with him…
Stagnant prof, I would highly recommend NOT taking this class with this prof at all costs. It's not too too hard to get a decent grade but he is a good person but with so hard exam.

Don't take this course with Kent! It is ridiculous that he tested so hard and made people feel like they had to study so hard and without direction. So many "In the class, we discussed about..." questions.

Although he is very nice I am not enjoying 275 due to the un-organization of the slides (mostly just pictures and not information of actual course concepts, which makes it difficult to review if you miss a class). I also find that politics are discussed way too much in the course even when its not related or relevant. Wouldn't take this prof again.

This professor is great, and he is new to the Beedie; he doesn't give any breaks in the class, and all the course is talking about Indigenous people and how to protect the environment (without any content with business). But overall, he is the good choice for this course.
